hero.title: Aperiodic generators.
hero.lede: Adjust settings to your liking and export the result.
hero.toolsText: Einstein, Spectre, and Penrose all support quick exports in SVG, PNG, and JPG.

cards.einsteinDescription: a single tile enforcing aperiodicity, rendered with various coloring modes and export options.
cards.spectreDescription: a single tile that allows enforcing aperiodicity without tile-flip, thus requiring more complex shapes
cards.penroseDescription: build classic Penrose tilings as kite-darts, rhombs, or a star-rich derived variant.

footer.lead: Aperiodos is a service for experiments in aperiodic tilings delivered by

layout.placeholder: Your generated artwork will appear here. Start with the default settings or explore a new palette.

common.colorPlaceholder: Type or search a CSS color

einstein.title: Einstein Generator
einstein.description: Adjust scale, size, and palette, then render a new image in the format you like.

spectre.title: Spectre Generator
spectre.description: Render bounded Spectre snapshots, choose a pattern variant, tune the palette and framing, and export SVG.

penrose.title: Penrose Generator
penrose.description: Generate Penrose tilings as kite-darts, rhombs, or a derived option that includes the star-heavy pentagonal motif from Penrose's first tiling.

hero.title: About Aperiodos
hero.summary: Aperiodos is a Trainvent subservice for aperiodic monotiles, image generation, and browser experiments. The site currently centers on monotiles, with plans to expand into other aperiodic patterns and Penrose tilings.

sections.creditsBody: This Trainvent subservice draws on papers, mathematical references, and public open-source experiments to explore how these tilings can be rendered and presented on the web.
sections.technicalBody: OpenAI helped with technical realization work across the project, including architecture planning, refactors, API shaping, and frontend/backend integration support.
sections.notesBody: The Rust-based Spectre work is being adapted into src/spectre_rs, with the older spectre clone kept as a reference source. Einstein and Spectre share one visual language inside the broader Trainvent web presence.
